name: El Golobar, Ujapero y Rojadillo — circular por Parque Natural Montaña Palentina
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Barruelo De Santullán traverse the mountainous terrain of Montaña Palentina, offering diverse landscapes for riders. The region features high mountain environments, shaped by rivers like the Carrión and Pisuerga, and extensive forests of beech, oak, and birch. Its rich mining heritage also contributes to unique routes through historical areas. This varied topography provides a range of challenges for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around Barruelo De Santullán The most popular mountain bike trail is Cordovilla de Aguilar a Brañosera circular desde Barruelo de Santullán — Parque Natural Montaña Palentina, a 25.3 miles (40.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 59 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the high mountain landscapes within the natural park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is El Golobar, Ujapero y Rojadillo — circular por Parque Natural Montaña Palentina, a difficult 33.4 miles (53.7 km) path. This route features significant elevation changes through forested areas and open mountain terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Corvio Necropolis – Golobar Refuge loop from Barruelo de Santullán, a 37.0 miles (59.5 km) trail leading through areas with historical mining heritage and natural park scenery, often completed in about 4 hours 26 minutes. Mountain biking around Barruelo De Santullán is defined by mountainous terrain, dense forests, and historical mining landscapes. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels. The routes in Barruelo De Santullán are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 140 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Barruelo De Santullán's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Barruelo De Santullán?
A: There are over 15 mountain bike trails around Barruelo De Santullán, offering a range of experiences from moderate to difficult. These routes traverse the diverse landscapes of Montaña Palentina, including high mountain environments, dense forests, and areas rich in mining heritage.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Barruelo De Santullán?
A: While our curated selection focuses on moderate to difficult routes, Barruelo de Santullán does offer options that can be suitable for families or less experienced riders. The region features mostly paved paths and less technical sections, such as the 'Senda de la Pedrosa', which is ideal for both walking and biking through picturesque oak groves. For a moderately challenging ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167879" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo Merino Waterfall loop from Barruelo de Santullán&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 25.9 km with significant elevation changes but offers rewarding scenery.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Barruelo De Santullán?
A: The terrain around Barruelo De Santullán is predominantly mountainous, characterized by the Montaña Palentina. You can expect significant elevation changes, with trails winding through extensive forests of beech, oak, and birch, as well as open mountain landscapes. Some routes also pass through areas with historical mining heritage, adding a unique dimension to the ride.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that feature waterfalls or other natural attractions?
A: Yes, you can find routes that lead to natural attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167879" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo Merino Waterfall loop from Barruelo de Santullán&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 25.9 km trail that takes you to a beautiful waterfall. Additionally, the nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3837834" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pisuerga River Source (Cueva del Cobre)&lt;/a&gt; is a significant natural feature in the region that can be explored.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest I can see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The region around Barruelo De Santullán is rich in historical and natural landmarks. You might encounter sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5648200" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corvio Necropolis&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40301575" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corvio Necropolis – Golobar Refuge loop from Barruelo de Santullán&lt;/a&gt;. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4848732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María la Real Monastery&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5786243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas de Pisuerga Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3306399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Cecilia&lt;/a&gt;, showcasing the area's Romanesque art and architecture.

Q: Are there any loop mountain bike trails around Barruelo De Santullán?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Barruelo De Santullán are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1157504009/cordovilla-de-aguilar-a-branosera-circular-desde-barruelo-de-santullan-parque-natural-montana-palentina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cordovilla de Aguilar a Brañosera circular desde Barruelo de Santullán — Parque Natural Montaña Palentina&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1176588576/el-golobar-ujapero-y-rojadillo-circular-por-parque-natural-montana-palentina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Golobar, Ujapero y Rojadillo — circular por Parque Natural Montaña Palentina&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20502261" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Bardal – Source of the Ebro River loop from Barruelo de Santullán&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Barruelo De Santullán?
A: The Montaña Palentina region, including Barruelo De Santullán, is best enjoyed for mountain biking during the spring, summer, and early autumn months. These seasons offer more stable weather conditions, less precipitation, and comfortable temperatures for exploring the mountainous terrain and forested areas. Winter can bring snow and colder temperatures, making some trails challenging or inaccessible.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Barruelo De Santullán?
A: The mountain bike trails around Barruelo De Santullán are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 140 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, from high mountain views to dense forests, and the unique experience of riding through areas with rich mining heritage. The variety of routes catering to different skill levels is also a frequently highlighted positive aspect.

Q: Are there any advanced mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Barruelo De Santullán offers several challenging routes for experienced mountain bikers. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1176588576/el-golobar-ujapero-y-rojadillo-circular-por-parque-natural-montana-palentina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Golobar, Ujapero y Rojadillo — circular por Parque Natural Montaña Palentina&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 53.7 km path with significant elevation changes, provide a robust workout amidst stunning natural scenery. The region's extensive network, including parts of the 'MTB Route Barruelo de Santullán-Balboa', also offers multi-stage expeditions for those seeking longer, more demanding adventures.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads in Barruelo De Santullán?
A: While specific parking information for each trailhead is best checked on individual route pages, Barruelo de Santullán, as a starting point for many routes, generally offers parking options within the town or at designated points near popular trail access areas. It's advisable to consult the komoot tour details for specific parking recommendations for your chosen route.

Q: Can I access mountain bike trails in Barruelo De Santullán using public transport?
A: Accessing some mountain bike trails directly via public transport might be limited in this mountainous region. While Barruelo de Santullán itself has some public transport connections, reaching specific, more remote trailheads often requires personal vehicle transport. For detailed information on public transport options to and from Barruelo de Santullán, it's best to check local bus schedules or regional transport services.
tips:
Town of the Palencia Mountain at the foot of the Sierra de la Braña. It is one of the highest towns in the province, being more than 1200 meters.
It is a pretty town where you can make a stop if you are in the area and visit the Romanesque church of San Martín Obispo.

This building was part of a project for a Parador de Turismo and ski resort subsidized by the Diputación de Palencia. To this day, the only ones who take advantage of it are the shepherds and their cattle that graze on those mountains.
Typical constructions in this area where a large rock was used to serve as a wall and roof and build the rest around it. Some are still used today as a mountain refuge.

waypoints.name: Church of San Martín Obispo de Salcedillo
